message:
fix(sync): cancellation-safe barrier, lost-wakeup prevention in Notify, and wake-under-lock elimination in Semaphore

barrier: Add early cancellation check before arrival registration so
that already-cancelled callers never increment the arrived counter. Add
a second checkpoint immediately after incrementing arrived -- if
cancellation races with registration, roll back the increment before
returning Cancelled. This prevents a subtle bug where the Nth (final)
arrival is cancelled: without the rollback, the barrier would trip
despite N-1 actual participants, because arrived==parties was satisfied
before the cancellation was detected. Removes stale comment about
whether to notify on cancel-after-arrive. Adds
barrier_cancelled_last_arrival_does_not_trip_generation test: spawns a
worker blocked at a 2-party barrier, attempts a cancelled second
arrival (verifies it returns Cancelled without tripping), then performs
a real second arrival and verifies exactly one leader is elected.

notify: Fix lost-wakeup bug in Notified::drop. When notify_one() marks
a waiter as notified but the future is dropped before it consumes the
notification (e.g. via select! or timeout), the notification was
silently lost. Now Drop checks the notified flag before removing the
waiter entry. If the waiter was marked notified, Drop performs
baton-passing: scan remaining waiters for the first active (unnotified,
with waker) entry, mark it notified, and wake it. If no active waiter
exists, re-store the notification to stored_notifications via
fetch_add(1, Release) so the next notified().await picks it up. The
waker is woken after dropping the lock to avoid wake-under-lock.

semaphore: Systematic elimination of wake-under-lock across all wakeup
paths. Extract two helpers: front_waiter_waker() (clones the front
waiter's waker while locked) and remove_waiter_and_take_next_waker()
(removes a waiter by id, returns the new front's waker if the removed
waiter was at the front). Both return Option<Waker> for the caller to
wake after the lock is released. Applied to: close() (drain wakers into
a Vec, drop lock, then wake all), add_permits() (clone front waker,
drop lock, then wake), AcquireFuture::drop and poll (cancel path and
successful-acquire path), OwnedAcquireFuture::drop and poll (same
pattern). Previously all these paths called wake_by_ref() while holding
the parking_lot::Mutex, which could deadlock if the waker callback
re-enters the semaphore (e.g. to check available_permits). Adds
ReentrantSemaphoreWaker test helper that calls available_permits()
inside wake_by_ref(), and add_permits_wakes_without_holding_lock test
that verifies the waker callback completes without deadlock.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
